title: tool_load — progressive disclosure gets the call right FIRST TRY
maps_to: Track A WS-1a — tool_load (MECHANISM test, white-box BY DESIGN)
persona: any — the MODEL is the subject
fixture: ⚠️ a book WITH a working ontology (kinds already adopted) — SKILL_BOOK_ID. propose_entities needs a `kind` to file the character under; on a kind-less book the call fails for a reason that has nothing to do with tool_load, and the test would measure the wrong thing.
_denylist_note: INTENTIONALLY EMPTY — white-box mechanism test (see S00a). The §1 vocabulary bar applies to black-box USER journeys, not to a primitive's own developer test.
canon_facts: the entity was actually created (DB, not the model's word)
the call SUCCEEDED first-try — no 'field required' validation round-trip from a guessed schema
tool_load EXECUTED NOTHING — it is disclosure, not a write

movements.label: Load one tool's schema, then call it correctly
Load a whole category at once
Call a loaded tool — no reload needed

turns.user: Add a character called Lâm Uyên to this book.
I'm going to do a bunch of glossary work — load the tools for it.
Now set her role to 'betrayed bride' and tell me what you saved.
